interpretation: Public subsidy attached to additional eligible primary/urgent/ambulance contacts.
public_input: NZ currently relies on capitation plus programme/ACC streams; no general MBS-style primary care benefits schedule exists.
source_name: Ministry of Health capitation reweighting page and ACC provider payment page
source_value: Capitation is core general practice funding; ACC pays activity/contract/regulation-based injury treatment.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.10 because marginal payment exists only partially via ACC/programmes and co-payments; benefits schedule scenarios increase this lever.
next_data_needed: Current capitation formula, ACC primary care claim volumes, programme funding and proposed benefit levels.

interpretation: Adequacy of capitation weights for need, rurality, deprivation and multimorbidity.
public_input: The current formula dates from 2002 and has had no major changes; reweighting will consider age, sex, multimorbidity, rurality and deprivation.
source_value: Formula introduced in 2002; new approach expected from 1 July 2026.
transform_to_normalised: Current pre-reweighting value set to 0.35; reweighting scenarios set to 0.75-0.85.
next_data_needed: Full formula/rate tables, implementation modelling, and practice-level revenue effects.

interpretation: Extent to which claimable primary care activity can be generated by providers acting within scope, not only GPs.
public_input: ACC recognises GP, nurse and nurse-practitioner contributions for injury-related treatment; broader policy model would extend scope-based contact benefits.
source_value: ACC contributes to consultation rates and procedures for GPs, nurses and nurse practitioners.
transform_to_normalised: Current value set to 0.35 to reflect partial scope use but GP-centred funding; scope-enabled scenarios set to 0.70-0.85.
next_data_needed: Provider workforce counts, scopes, prescribing rules, substitution studies, clinical governance models.

interpretation: Friction, opacity or entry barrier from PHO-mediated funding and contracting.
public_input: Current architecture still relies on PHO/service-agreement processes; transparency and pass-through issues require further evidence.
source_name: Health NZ primary care health data; prior PHO review artefacts in repo
source_value: Primary care data governance and SLM processes involve PHOs; closed-books availability is reported as a primary care indicator.
transform_to_normalised: Current set to 0.60; direct-claiming scenarios reduce to 0.15-0.30.
next_data_needed: PHO financial flows, pass-through rules, provider onboarding timelines, direct claims costings.

interpretation: Political and managerial visibility of primary care access and outcomes.
public_input: A primary care target will take effect from 1 July 2026, proposed as >80% access within one week.
source_name: Ministry of Health primary care health target
source_value: >80% of people can access an appointment with a general practice provider within one week.
transform_to_normalised: Set current transitional value to 0.40 because target is announced but still being implemented; full architecture 0.85.
next_data_needed: Final target definition, balancing measures, reporting frequency and consequence management.

interpretation: Visibility of ambulance response, demand, non-conveyance and alternative disposition outcomes.
public_input: Ambulance providers report KPI data, including response times, demand, quality and call types, to the Ambulance Team.
source_value: Monthly reports show response time KPIs, demand, quality of service and call type.
transform_to_normalised: Current set to 0.45 because ambulance KPIs exist but are not treated as hospital-equivalent top-tier system KPIs.
next_data_needed: Alternative disposition, non-conveyance safety, offload delay and top-tier reporting status.

interpretation: Relative penalty for visible hospital failure compared with upstream access failure.
public_input: Health NZ plans and commissions hospital, primary and community services; hospitals remain highly visible operationally.
source_name: Ministry of Health Crown entities page
source_value: Health NZ plans and commissions hospital, primary and community services.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.90 as a strategic judgement; reduced only where upstream KPIs are top-tier and data visible.
next_data_needed: Budget decisions, media analysis, board papers, ministerial escalation patterns.

interpretation: Ability to observe appointment access, unmet need, ambulance pathways, ED flow and avoidable hospitalisation links.
public_input: NPCD begins with general practice encounter and appointment data, including booking, seen date and outcome, from April 2026.
source_name: Health NZ National Primary Care Dataset
source_value: Participating practices share encounter and appointment data from April 2026; first focus is the seven-day access target.
transform_to_normalised: Set transitional current to 0.45; full architecture 0.85 with linked primary, ambulance and hospital data.
next_data_needed: Actual data completeness, PMS coverage, linkage to ED/ambulance/ASH and timeliness.

interpretation: Patient price signal/private contribution to primary care utilisation.
public_input: In 2024/25, 14.9% of adults reported not visiting a GP because of cost; Pacific and Māori adults reported higher cost barriers.
source_value: Adults cost barrier 14.9%; Pacific adults 25.1%; Māori adults 18.7%.
transform_to_normalised: Normalise national adult cost barrier against a 25% high-burden benchmark: 14.9/25 ~= 0.60.
next_data_needed: Practice fee schedules, fee caps, CSC/VLCA eligibility and utilisation response by deprivation/ethnicity/rurality.

interpretation: Extent of targeted protection from co-payment barriers.
public_input: Cost barriers remain material despite existing protections; Pacific, Māori and disabled adults have higher barriers.
source_value: Cost barrier persisted at 14.9% nationally; Pacific 25.1%, Māori 18.7%; disabled adults had more access barriers.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.45; full architecture 0.80 reflecting stronger concessions, caps and zero-fee contact types.
next_data_needed: Distribution of fees and unmet need by group, patient-level frequent-user protection data.

interpretation: Strength of kaupapa Māori, Pacific, outreach, locality, navigation and trust functions.
public_input: Access barriers are higher for several high-need groups; equity functions must be preserved if direct benefits reduce intermediation.
source_value: Pacific adults had highest GP cost barrier; disabled adults had more barriers including owing money, transport and support person.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.60, reflecting existing but insufficient equity infrastructure; full architecture 0.85.
next_data_needed: Kaupapa Māori/Pacific provider capacity, programme funding, outcomes by ethnicity/deprivation/disability/rurality.

interpretation: Extent of telehealth availability.
public_input: Online and phone access are part of current Health NZ service pathways, but scale and substitution effects require data.
source_value: Online GP Care is mentioned as a performance-reporting example; exact volumes not yet in public calibration pack.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.65; loose benefit scenario 0.90; full architecture keeps scale but improves integration.
next_data_needed: Telehealth volumes, rural uptake, substitution vs complementarity, continuity and safety outcomes.

interpretation: Integration of telehealth with local teams, records and in-person escalation.
public_input: NPCD and Shared Digital Health Record work may improve integration, but data maturity is still developing.
source_name: Health NZ primary care health data
source_value: Shared Digital Health Record and NPCD are being developed; participation and data processes are evolving.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.45; full architecture 0.80.
next_data_needed: Record-linkage coverage, escalation pathways, continuity metrics, safety events.

interpretation: Payment support for local and rural in-person capacity.
public_input: Time to get an appointment is the largest reported GP barrier; rural/local in-person supply is a policy concern but not fully quantified here.
source_value: 25.5% of adults reported appointment wait time as a barrier.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.25 given persistent wait-time barrier; full architecture 0.80 with explicit local/in-person loadings.
next_data_needed: Rurality, travel time, local procedural/exam-dependent service data and practice viability by region.

interpretation: Funding for hear-and-treat, treat-and-refer, non-conveyance and alternative destination pathways.
public_input: Ambulance is commissioned by Health NZ and ACC; medical patients funded by Health NZ and injury patients by ACC.
source_value: Emergency ambulance services are commissioned on behalf of Health NZ and ACC; medical and injury funding split.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.35; ambulance-alternative scenario and full architecture to 0.75-0.80.
next_data_needed: Ambulance disposition data, non-conveyance safety, follow-up funding, ED avoidance rates.

interpretation: Extent to which ACC activity payments stabilise primary/urgent care marginal supply.
public_input: ACC pays based on regulations, contracts or purchase orders, including consultation and procedure contributions for GP/nurse/NP services.
source_value: ACC contributes to consultation rates and specific treatment/procedure costs for GPs, nurses and nurse practitioners.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.70; ACC constraint shock lowers to 0.45.
next_data_needed: Practice revenue mix, claim volumes, provider response to ACC pricing, injury/non-injury cross-subsidy.

interpretation: Degree of policy/pricing constraint on ACC activity funding.
public_input: ACC provider payments are rules-based and activity-sensitive; constraining them may have cross-system effects.
source_value: How ACC pays depends on service type and whether provider works under regulations, contract or purchase order.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.10; ACC constraint shock 0.60; full architecture 0.00-0.05.
next_data_needed: Pricing changes, claim caps, provider exit/entry response, cross-funder spillover.

interpretation: Constraint imposed by fixed envelopes, baselines and deficit pressure.
public_input: Health NZ plans/commissions hospitals, primary and community services; the model hypothesises upstream growth competes with hospital pressure.
source_name: Ministry of Health Crown entities page
source_value: Health NZ is responsible for planning and commissioning hospital, primary and community health services.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.85; benefit-stream scenarios lower effective tightness because activity can expand within rules.
next_data_needed: Vote/baseline data, spending trends by service class, deficit response and reprioritisation evidence.

interpretation: Clinical governance, credentialing, prescribing scope and audit for scope-enabled claiming.
public_input: ACC requires appropriate qualifications/current practising certificate and acceptable quality/right time/place treatment.
source_value: ACC payment conditions include appropriate qualifications, current practising certificate, quality and documentation.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.75; full architecture 0.90; loose benefits 0.40.
next_data_needed: Credentialing model, prescribing/standing-order rules, adverse events, audit capacity.

interpretation: Audit, coding rules and balancing measures to prevent low-value activity and target gaming.
public_input: NPCD and target governance include balancing measures; target design still needs confirmation.
source_name: Health NZ primary care health data
source_value: Advisory/governance groups will advise target definition, implementation and balancing measures.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.60; full architecture 0.85; loose benefits 0.25.
next_data_needed: Coding rules, utilisation outliers, fraud/audit design, balancing measures, quality indicators.

interpretation: Ability for accredited providers to claim directly through a national rules-based platform.
public_input: Current funding remains mediated through capitation/PHO/service arrangements; direct national claiming is the proposed architecture.
source_name: Ministry of Health capitation; Health NZ primary care data
source_value: Capitation is not paid directly to patients; PHO/governance structures remain central to data/funding arrangements.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.05; direct-claims scenarios 0.75-0.90.
next_data_needed: Platform specifications, credentialing, claims processing cost, provider onboarding and audit data.

interpretation: Coalition support across providers, PHOs, Māori/Pacific providers, ambulance, Health NZ, ACC and Treasury.
public_input: No direct public calibration yet; stakeholder validation is required.
source_value: Not yet measured.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.35; improved where reform is coherent and preserves equity/locality functions.
next_data_needed: Workshop scoring by stakeholder group; submissions; interviews.

interpretation: Clarity of reform narrative as access architecture rather than pro-market or sector income claim.
public_input: No direct public calibration yet; communication testing is required.
source_value: Not yet measured.
transform_to_normalised: Set current to 0.30; full architecture 0.85 if framed as access/hospital-avoidance and equity-protected.
next_data_needed: Stakeholder/political discourse analysis and messaging tests.
